name: Reusable Heavy-Lift Rockets
what: Fully or partially reusable boosters that land and refly, collapsing the cost of reaching orbit by an order of magnitude versus expendable rockets.
how: First stages perform propulsive landings on pads or drone ships, are inspected and refuelled, and fly again within days to weeks.

name: LEO Broadband Constellations
what: Thousands of small satellites in low Earth orbit delivering broadband to under-served regions with latency competitive with terrestrial fibre.
how: Mass-produced flat-pack satellites are launched in batches, phased-array antennas track user terminals, and inter-satellite laser links route traffic in orbit.

name: Reusable Lunar Landers
use: Crew and cargo to the lunar surface
what: Human-rated landers designed to ferry crew and cargo between lunar orbit and the surface as part of a sustained Moon presence.
how: Cryogenic or storable-propellant stages dock with an orbiting gateway, descend under throttled engines, and are refuelled for reuse.

name: Nuclear Thermal Propulsion
what: Reactors that heat propellant to far higher exhaust velocities than chemical rockets, roughly halving transit times to Mars.
how: A fission core heats liquid hydrogen to extreme temperatures and expels it through a nozzle, doubling the efficiency of chemical propulsion.

name: Space Launch Technologies
what: Advancements in rocket technology and launch systems.
how: Enabling more frequent and cost-effective satellite launches.

name: Satellite Constellations for Internet Access
what: Deploy large numbers of small satellites into low Earth orbit.
how: Provide continuous and reliable internet coverage worldwide.

name: Lunar Base Construction Technologies
use: Building and maintaining habitats on the Moon
what: Developing technologies for long-term lunar habitation.
how: Using 3D printing with lunar regolith to construct structures.

name: Mars Rover Missions
use: Advanced rovers for long-term Martian surface missions
what: Equipping rovers with sophisticated scientific instruments.
how: Sending advanced robotic explorers to study the Martian environment and prepare for human missions.

name: Launch Services
what: Development of reusable rockets for commercial satellite launches.

name: Orbital Rings
what: Orbital rings are massive structures built in Earth's orbit to facilitate long-term habitation and resource extraction.
how: These rings use advanced materials and construction techniques to create stable, self-sustaining habitats.

name: Space Launch System for Commercial Use
what: Enables more frequent and cost-effective launches.
how: Reduces barriers to entry in the space industry.

name: Satellite Constellations for Global Connectivity
use: Providing global internet access and IoT connectivity
what: Expands the reach of satellite networks.
how: Improves coverage and reliability of communication services.

name: Mars Rover Missions for Scientific Exploration
use: Collecting data and samples from Mars
what: Advances our understanding of the Red Planet.
how: Facilitates long-term scientific research and potential colonization.

name: Reusable Launch Vehicles
what: Developing reusable rocket technology to reduce the cost of space access.
how: Using advanced materials and aerodynamics for reusability.

name: Satellite Constellations
use: Deploying large networks of satellites to provide global internet coverage and other services.
what: These constellations will enable high-speed, low-latency connectivity in remote areas and support various applications such as navigation, weather monitoring, and Earth observation.
how: Satellite manufacturers are developing smaller, more efficient spacecraft with longer lifespans.

name: Mars Exploration Missions
use: Planning and executing missions to explore Mars for scientific research and potential human habitation.
what: These missions aim to understand the Martian environment, search for signs of past or present life, and pave the way for future colonization efforts.
how: Space agencies are developing advanced rovers, landers, and orbiters equipped with sophisticated instruments.

name: Space Launch Technologies for Commercial Use
what: Advancements in launch technologies enable more frequent and cost-effective commercial space missions.
how: Facilitating the growth of the commercial space industry.

name: Mars Exploration Missions for Human Settlement
what: Exploring Mars to pave the way for human settlement and scientific discovery.
how: Understanding the planet's resources and potential for colonization.
